expectations.text: Guide follows four-segment structure (Opening, Warm-up, Core Discovery, Wrap-up)
expectations.evidence: Four segments present: SEGMENT 1: OPENING, SEGMENT 2: WARM-UP, SEGMENT 3: CORE DISCOVERY, SEGMENT 4: WRAP-UP

expectations.text: Questions ask about past behaviour, not hypothetical futures
expectations.evidence: Core discovery opens with 'walk me through the last time you brought someone new onto your team'. No hypothetical questions in discovery.

expectations.text: No mention of the proposed onboarding feature in the first half of the guide
expectations.evidence: No mention of product feature in Segments 1-3. Interviewer Reminders explicitly state 'Do NOT mention the onboarding feature idea until after Core Discovery'.

expectations.text: Workaround probe is included
expectations.evidence: Explicit workaround probe: 'Before you had your current process... how did you handle it? And do you still use any part of that earlier approach today?'

expectations.text: Note-taking template includes Key observations, Notable quotes, Surprises, Follow-up questions
expectations.evidence: All four fields present in template plus bonus 'Workarounds in use' field.

expectations.text: Guide specifies participant criteria and target sample size
expectations.evidence: Header: 'People managers and HR/People Ops leads at companies with 50-500 employees who have onboarded at least 2 new hires in the past 6 months; n=8-10 target'

expectations.text: Guide follows four-segment structure (Opening, Warm-up, Core Discovery, Wrap-up)
expectations.evidence: Uses six sections: Introduction, Background, Current Onboarding Process, Pain Points, Feature Exploration, Wrap-Up.

expectations.text: Questions ask about past behaviour, not hypothetical futures
expectations.evidence: Multiple hypothetical questions: 'What would an ideal onboarding experience look like?', 'Would you find it helpful to have automated task assignments?', 'Would you pay for a tool?'

expectations.text: No mention of the proposed onboarding feature in the first half of the guide
expectations.evidence: Section 4 explicitly states 'We're considering building a team onboarding feature' — introduces solution before full problem exploration.

expectations.text: Workaround probe is included
expectations.evidence: No workaround probe anywhere in the guide.

expectations.text: Note-taking template includes Key observations, Notable quotes, Surprises, Follow-up questions
expectations.evidence: No note-taking template. Only generic reminders like 'Record responses carefully'.

expectations.text: Guide specifies participant criteria and target sample size
expectations.evidence: Target listed as 'HR managers, team leads, people ops at mid-market companies' — no specific criteria or sample size.

expectations.text: Questions are appropriate for C-level stakeholders
expectations.evidence: Questions at strategic level: 'month-end or quarter-end', 'cost of that delay', budget allocation probes. No granular tactical questions.

expectations.text: Duration is appropriate for executive availability (30-45 minutes)
expectations.evidence: Duration set to '30 minutes (executive-appropriate)'. Segments total 28-32 minutes.

expectations.text: Core discovery focuses on reporting workflow pain, not feature wish lists
expectations.evidence: Discovery opens with 'walk me through the last time you needed a report our product couldn't give you'. No feature wish-list questions in discovery.

expectations.text: Includes probes for budget and decision-making authority
expectations.evidence: Explicit 'Budget and decision-making probe' with 3 questions. Note-taking template includes 'Budget signals' field.

expectations.text: The five design principles are followed
expectations.evidence: All five principles in INTERVIEWER REMINDERS: Behaviour over opinion, Past over hypothetical, Problem before solution, Silence is data, Why five times.

expectations.text: Questions are appropriate for C-level stakeholders
expectations.evidence: Too granular: 'How often do you generate reports?', satisfaction on 1-10 scale, 'What's your budget?' too blunt for executive discovery.

expectations.text: Duration is appropriate for executive availability (30-45 minutes)
expectations.evidence: Duration set to 60 minutes — too long for CFOs.

expectations.text: Core discovery focuses on reporting workflow pain, not feature wish lists
expectations.evidence: Part 4 is a feature wish list: 'What specific customizations?', 'self-service report building or built for you?', 'ideal reporting dashboard?'

expectations.text: Includes probes for budget and decision-making authority
expectations.evidence: Q15 and Q16 address purchasing decisions and budget, though bluntly.

expectations.text: The five design principles are followed
expectations.evidence: Multiple violations: opinion questions (1-10 scale), hypothetical questions, solution before problem, no silence guidance, no 'why' probes.

notes: Skill achieves 100% pass rate across both evals (11/11 assertions passed)
Without skill, pass rate is 9% (1/11 assertions passed — only 'budget probes' in eval 2)
The skill's strongest differentiator is structural: four-segment format, workaround probes, note-taking templates, and participant criteria are never produced without the skill

The five design principles (behaviour over opinion, past over hypothetical, problem before solution, silence is data, why five times) are consistently followed with-skill and consistently violated without-skill
Without-skill outputs default to feature wish-list and hypothetical questions — the exact anti-patterns the skill guards against
Duration adaptation is a notable differentiator: with-skill correctly shortened to 30 min for executives, without-skill defaulted to 60 min regardless
Timing and token data unavailable — runs were executed inline rather than via subagents
